articles.title: Emotion Recognition Accuracy Across Nonverbal Modalities: Associations with Working Memory
articles.authors: Peyton Nault, Michele Morningstar
articles.abstract: Recognizing emotional intent from prosody (vocal emotion recognition [ER]) is found to be more challenging than the same ability with facial expressions (facial ER). However, disparities between these nonverbal modalities may be because most research compares ER accuracy for static faces and dynamic prosody. Interpreting dynamic expressions that unfold over time may be more difficult and require more advanced cognitive abilities, like working memory. We explored (a) differences in ER accuracy across tasks varying in modality (facial, vocal) and dynamism (static, dynamic), and (b) whether trait-level cognitive abilities (working memory, attentional control) differentially predicted ER accuracy across tasks. 300 adults (52.3% women) completed ER tasks (static voice [vocal bursts], static face, dynamic voice [prosody], dynamic face), working memory tasks (backwards digit span, N-back), and an attentional control task (antisaccade) online. A linear mixed-effects model of the effect of modality, dynamism, working memory, and attentional control on accuracy revealed that dynamic facial expressions were recognized more accurately than vocal prosody, but static faces and vocal bursts were recognized with similar accuracy. Better performance on the N-back task was linked to higher accuracy on ER tasks in the facial modality. Findings clarify the relative complexity of ER across nonverbal modalities and identify potential cognitive correlates of this important socio-emotional skill.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Nonverbal communication; Working memory; Cognitive psychology; Emotion recognition; Developmental psychology; Recognition memory; Negative emotion; Recall
articles.category: Emotion, Facial Expression, Vocal Expression, Emotion Perception
articles.summary: 本研究探讨了情绪识别在非言语模态（面部和声音）中的准确性差异，以及与工作记忆的关联。实验比较了静态和动态面部及声音刺激，发现动态面部表情的识别准确性高于声音韵律，而静态面部和声音爆发准确性相似；同时，工作记忆能力与面部情绪识别准确性正相关。这些发现澄清了跨模态情绪识别的相对复杂性，并揭示了认知因素在社会情绪技能中的作用。

articles.title: The Effect of Medical Face Masks on First Impressions Across Race and Time: Testing Social and Perceptual Explanations
articles.authors: R. Thora Bjornsdottir, John Paul Wilson, Sanjay Chhetri
articles.abstract: A growing body of research shows medical face masks to affect face perception and recognition. Although face masks negatively impact recognition of face identities and emotion expressions, they largely positively affect trait impressions. Here, focusing on impressions of trustworthiness and responsibility, we bridge between various lines of inquiry in the extant literature. First, among American perceivers, we test whether the effect of masks varies by target faces' race, crucially including targets from three racial groups: Asian, Black, and White. Next, we examine the extent to which effects can be explained by the presence of masks versus the absence of lower facial information. Finally, we examine the role of temporal context, testing masks' effect on trustworthiness and responsibility judgments in early 2021 and mid 2023. Altogether, we find face masks to have a positive effect on trustworthiness and responsibility judgments. Lack of lower facial information explained some, but not all, of the effect of masking, indicating that masks provide a specific social signal that affects trait judgments beyond the effect of occlusion. Finally, we found the positive effect of masking on trait judgments to persist in 2023 but decrease in magnitude compared to 2021, indicating that the social meaning of masks has waned over time. Masking effects were moreover stronger for White than Black (2021, 2023) and Asian targets (2023) and among perceivers with more positive attitudes toward masking (especially in 2023). Altogether, we provide evidence that both bottom-up perceptual and top-down social factors influence social perceptions of masked faces.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Trustworthiness; Perception; Trait; Masking; Social psychology; Social perception; Affect; Face perception; Impression formation
articles.category: Emotion, Affect, Face, Social Cognition, Impression
articles.summary: 本研究考察了医疗口罩对信任和责任第一印象的影响，发现口罩总体上提升了积极印象，其效应部分源于感知因素（如缺少下半脸信息），部分源于社会信号，且随时间推移而减弱，同时存在种族差异和感知者态度的作用。

articles.title: AI-generated face images of emotional expressions
articles.authors: Robin S. S. Kramer, Natália Javorková, Peter R. Clark
articles.abstract: Recently developed artificial intelligence (AI) tools are now capable of generating realistic face images that are indistinguishable from photographs. However, little is known regarding how these synthetic images portray emotional expressions. Here, with only simple prompting, we generated a set of images depicting seven facial expressions using ChatGPT. These images were intermixed with posed facial expression photographs drawn from a standardised database and we collected participants' judgements regarding which emotions were portrayed, along with their rated intensities. Our results showed that synthetic images were more accurately classified as their intended emotions, as well as more intense displays, in comparison with posed photographs. In addition, AI-generated images depicted action units associated with particular emotions to a similar extent to posed photos, although this pattern varied across emotions. Finally, both types of images were correctly classified by ChatGPT with few or no errors. Taken together, these findings provide insight into how AI tools represent, and subsequently depict, human emotions. Further, this approach may facilitate the creation of novel, individually-tailored image sets for use in future research by incorporating more complex and specific prompting.
articles.keywords: Facial expression; Set; Face; Artificial intelligence; Psychology; Image; Action; Expression; Emotional expression
articles.summary: 本研究利用ChatGPT生成描绘七种情绪的面部表情图像，并与标准化数据库中的摆拍照片混合，收集参与者对情绪分类和强度的判断。结果显示，AI生成的图像更准确地被分类为预期情绪，且强度更高。这些发现为AI工具如何表示和描绘人类情绪提供了新见解，展示了AI在情绪表达研究中的潜在应用。

articles.title: Emotional expression in deaf and hearing populations
articles.authors: Rose Stamp, Dean Geckt, Liran Evenberg, Svetlana Dachkovsky, Wendy Sandler, Hagit Hel-Or
articles.abstract: The expression of emotion is a crucial part of everyday social interaction. This may be especially true of deaf populations. Studies show that the perception of emotions can differ between deaf and hearing people. On the one hand, sign language knowledge may contribute to more accurate identification of facial expressions (Goldstein & Feldman, 1996). On the other hand, deaf people can experience delays in emotional perception, as a result of delays in language exposure (Martins et al., 2019; Ziv et al., 2013). But no study to date has compared the production of emotional expressions by deaf and hearing adults. Our study is the first of its kind with the goal of determining specifically whether sign language knowledge and deaf experience lead to differences in the production of emotional expression of deaf and hearing individuals. We recruited 45 participants, representing three groups: (1) hearing non-signers (speaking), (2) hearing signers (in two conditions, signing and speaking), and (3) deaf signers (signing). The participants performed a language-related task eliciting emotional sentences in which the emotion and its expression were congruent. Our study is unique because we used facial tracking technology and machine learning techniques to describe and distinguish the four emotional conditions produced — sadness, happiness, anger and neutral. The results of the study show that deaf and hearing individuals differ in systematic ways, demonstrating that each group can be classified accurately based on their emotional expressions.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Facial expression; Emotional expression; Anger; Sign language; Perception; Expression; Identification; Emotion perception
articles.category: Emotion, Facial Expression, Emotion Perception, Emotion Expression, Face, AI
articles.summary: 本研究首次比较了聋人和听人在情绪表达产生方面的差异。通过面部跟踪技术和机器学习分析悲伤、快乐、愤怒和中性四种情绪条件，发现两组在情绪表达上存在系统差异，每组均可根据表情准确分类。

articles.title: Underestimating the gratitude of strangers: how givers pessimistically predict recipients' gratitude for small acts of kindness
articles.authors: Yuma Shiraki
articles.abstract: Gratitude serves as positive feedback for givers of kind acts, reinforcing their motivation for further such acts. However, this gratitude-kindness cycle depends on givers' perception of recipients' gratitude—a stage that has received little empirical attention. The present research examined whether givers of small acts of kindness to strangers underestimate recipients' gratitude. Across three scenario-based studies, I found consistent evidence of gratitude underestimation. Study 1 revealed that givers predicted lower gratitude than recipients actually experienced, regardless of the type of kindness. Study 2 suggested that this underestimation was attributable to givers' pessimistic inference rather than recipients' overreporting. Study 3 further showed that this tendency disappeared when recipients verbally expressed their gratitude. These findings suggest that givers' perception of recipients' gratitude constitutes a bottleneck in the prosocial cycle, potentially undermining their prosocial motivation. Importantly, this bottleneck can be resolved through simple verbal acknowledgment. Exploratory mediation analyses did not yield consistent effects, suggesting that the mechanism underlying underestimation warrants further investigation.
articles.keywords: Gratitude; Psychology; Kindness; Social psychology; Developmental psychology; Cognitive psychology; Personality; Mindfulness
articles.summary: 本研究通过三个场景实验探讨了给予者对接受者感恩的低估现象。结果发现，给予者预测的感恩程度低于接受者实际体验，这种偏差源于给予者的悲观推断而非接受者过度报告，且当接受者言语表达感恩时低估会消失。这表明对他人情绪的感知偏差可能阻碍亲社会动机，但简单的口头认可能有效缓解。

articles.title: Under pressure: The role of supplemental work for cross-domain spillover of psychological need frustration
articles.authors: Ferdinand Baierer, Lucas Alexander Maunz, Jürgen Glaser
articles.abstract: Research on supplemental work has yielded mixed findings, linking it to both ill-being (e.g., exhaustion and recovery impairments) and potential benefits (e.g., increased performance and psychological detachment). What remains unclear are the processes that elicit supplemental work and how it affects ill-being. Drawing on self-determination theory (SDT), we develop a dynamic spillover framework that positions supplemental work as a behavior elicited by basic psychological need frustration (NF) in the work domain. We propose that supplemental work carries costs by increasing NF in the non-work domain, thereby disrupting cross-domain need balance and ultimately increasing next-day exhaustion at work. Results from an experience sampling study (141 employees, 2440 observations) supported our hypotheses: within-person increases in work overload predicted subsequent NF in the work domain and supplemental work. Competence frustration, in particular, triggered subsequent supplemental work that was associated with increased NF in the non-work domain and higher next-day exhaustion. These findings add to the notion of a detrimental pathway of supplemental work and highlight the importance of dynamic, cross-domain need frustration processes in understanding its consequences for employee well-being.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Frustration; Work; Spillover effect; Social psychology; Competence; Experience sampling method; Self-determination theory; Burnout
articles.summary: 本研究基于自我决定理论构建了一个动态溢出模型，将补充工作视为工作领域基本心理需求挫折引发的行为。通过141名员工的经验取样数据，研究发现补充工作会增加非工作领域的需求挫折，从而加剧次日的工作耗竭，尤其当能力受挫时。这揭示了补充工作有害影响的机制，并强调了跨领域需求挫折的动态过程。

articles.title: The mental price of losing control: Rewards and willingness to invest mental effort under conditions of uncontrollability
articles.authors: Jakub Michalik, Marcin Bukowski, Bartosz Majchrowicz, Kerstin Fröber
articles.abstract: Perceiving a lack of control over one's environment can impair cognitive performance and may reduce willingness to invest cognitive effort. Across two experiments ( N = 624), we tested whether experimentally induced uncontrollability decreases subsequent effort-based choice and whether monetary rewards offset this effect. Uncontrollability was induced using Behavioral Helplessness Training, and effort-based choice was assessed in a Voluntary Task Switching paradigm in which participants could choose between repeating a task or switching to an alternative. The Voluntary Switch Rate served as the primary behavioral index of choosing the option treated as more cognitively demanding, that is, as requiring greater cognitive control. We used a shape/location variant in Experiment 1 and a letter/number variant in Experiment 2 with double registration and 20% invalid trials to better match task difficulty, separate choice from execution, and induce a degree of environmental unpredictability. Rewards robustly increased switching in both experiments. In Experiment 1, low control attenuated sensitivity to rewards. In Experiment 2, uncontrollability reduced switching overall and increased the reward magnitude required to justify switching. Exploratory mediation analyses further suggested that reduced motivation and increased negative affect mediated the link between uncontrollability and switching behavior. Overall, these results advance our understanding of motivational processes underlying helplessness and provide implications for the design of interventions aimed at sustaining cognitive engagement under uncontrollable conditions.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Learned helplessness; Cognition; Task; Affect; Mediation; Social psychology; Psychological intervention; Task switching
articles.summary: 本研究通过两个实验考察了实验诱导的不可控性对认知努力选择的影响，发现货币奖励能增强任务切换行为，但不可控性会降低个体对奖励的敏感性，并减少整体切换率。中介分析表明，动机下降和负面情感增加在不可控性与切换行为之间起介导作用。这些结果深化了对无助状态下动机过程的理解，并为维持认知参与提供了干预设计启示。

articles.title: More materialistic by escaping experiences: experiential avoidance mediates the link between attachment anxiety and material values
articles.authors: Emrullah Ecer, Agata Gasiorowska, Tobias Otterbring
articles.abstract: People with attachment anxiety frequently experience problems in social relationships and tend to form a strong attachment toward material objects as a substitute for their interpersonal insecurities. However, the underlying mechanism linking attachment anxiety with material values is not well-defined. We propose that anxiously attached people are more prone to avoid thoughts, feelings, and experiences, thereby leading to greater material values. In three correlational studies and a preregistered experiment conducted across Russian, Turkish, Polish, and U.S. samples (N = 1,397), we investigated whether experiential avoidance mediates the link between attachment anxiety and material values. The indirect effect of attachment anxiety on material values through experiential avoidance was consistently significant across all studies. In Study 4, we also demonstrated that inducing attachment anxiety (vs. negative affect) led to a suppression of unwanted experiences, feelings, and thoughts, resulting in more material values. We suggest that interventions focused on secure relationships may enhance anxiously attached individuals' action strategies, potentially reducing their material values, with implications for the literature on attachment styles and material values as well as broader models of social and clinical psychology.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Experiential avoidance; Materialism; Anxiety; Social psychology; Link; Experiential learning; Developmental psychology
articles.summary: 本研究探讨了依恋焦虑如何通过经验回避中介影响物质价值观。跨文化样本的相关研究和实验表明，依恋焦虑的间接效应通过经验回避始终显著，诱发依恋焦虑（而非负面情感）会导致对不想要经验、感受和想法的抑制，从而增强物质价值观。

articles.title: Evaluating emotion effects on episodic memory: what makes negative information memorable?
articles.authors: Tianyu Amber Hu, Kimele Persaud
articles.abstract: The emotional enhancement effect of memory (EEM), evidenced by the prioritization of negative over neutral information in episodic memory, has primarily been attributed to arousal- and valence-based mechanisms. Recent research, however, has provided evidence suggesting that arousal and valence alone are not sufficient to explain more nuanced emotion memory effects. Here, we proposed that the survival relevance of different affective information could also contribute to the enhancing effects of emotion on memory. In two studies, we assessed whether individuals' single-item and associative memory differed for negative information that varied in survival relevance (i.e., threat- and disgust-related images versus annoyance-related images) and neutral information. We found partial evidence that survival relevance selectively increased recognition accuracy, specifically for disgust-related items and the paired contexts associated with threat-related items. These findings emphasize the utility of exploring additional mechanisms (e.g., survival relevance) that work in concert with arousal and valence to further elucidate the processes that underlie the interaction between emotion and memory.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Arousal; Valence; Relevance; Cognitive psychology; Episodic memory; Associative property; Negative information; Two-factor theory of emotion; Emotional valence
articles.summary: 本研究评估了情绪对情景记忆的增强效应，发现负面信息的记忆优先性不仅由唤醒和效价机制驱动，信息的生存相关性（如威胁和厌恶）也能选择性提升识别准确性，特别是在厌恶相关项目和威胁相关上下文的配对记忆中，强调了多机制协同作用的重要性。

articles.title: Boredom over time: the interaction between achievement goals and perceived classroom goal structures in Taiwanese mathematics classrooms
articles.authors: Shu-Ling Peng, Hsin-Ling Chen, Biing-Lin Cherng
articles.abstract: Boredom is a discrete achievement emotion with important downstream consequences, yet longitudinal evidence on how personal goals and perceived classroom goal structures (CGS) jointly shape boredom remains scarce. Addressing this gap, this study tests time-lagged links from achievement goals to subsequent boredom and the reverse paths, and examines whether these dynamics vary by students' perceived CGS. Using three waves of data from 726 Taiwanese seventh graders, we estimated four 2 × 2 achievement goal cross-lagged panel models (CLPMs) to test reciprocal associations, followed by multi-group comparisons (mastery-oriented vs. performance-oriented CGS) to evaluate the moderating role of the perceived CGS. Results showed short-term reciprocal associations between mastery-approach goals and boredom, and stable effects whereby boredom predicted increases in performance-avoidance goals and decreases in mastery-approach goals over time. Multi-group models indicated that, relative to a performance-oriented CGS, a mastery-oriented CGS amplified the boredom-reducing effect of mastery-approach goals and exacerbated the boredom-enhancing effect of performance-avoidance goals. These findings underscore the importance of aligning students' personal goals with classroom climates to mitigate boredom and demonstrate how motivation and context jointly shape students' emotions over time.
articles.keywords: Boredom; Psychology; Reciprocal; Context; Social psychology; Test; Developmental psychology; Dynamics; Structural equation modeling; Longitudinal study
articles.summary: 本研究通过纵向设计考察台湾数学课堂中成就目标与无聊情绪之间的相互影响，以及感知的课堂目标结构在其中的调节作用。结果发现，掌握接近目标与无聊存在短期双向关联，而无聊对目标有稳定影响；掌握导向的课堂环境能放大掌握接近目标对无聊的减少效应，并加剧绩效回避目标对无聊的增强效应。

articles.title: Moral anger, disgust, but also sadness: examining the victim vs. perpetrator focus in the moral evaluation of transgressions
articles.authors: Eva Vives, Atlas Thébault Guiochon, Bastien Trémolière, Agnès Falco, Hakim Djeriouat
articles.abstract: In two preregistered studies (N = 312), we aimed to test whether inducing participants' attention towards the perpetrator or victim of a moral violation would trigger different emotional responses. Fictional situations were created to focus either on the victim's (e.g., a person being tripped on the street) or the perpetrator's experience (e.g., a person tripping another). Participants were presented with a series of vignettes describing moral transgressions and asked to judge as quickly as possible whether the emotional reactions of third parties depicted in different facial expressions were consistent with what would be expected regarding the misconduct. We hypothesized that focusing on the perpetrator would stress their poor moral character and elicit distancing reactions typical of moral disgust, while focusing on the victim would emphasize the harmful act and the victim's resulting personal distress, aligning more closely with moral anger. Results provided only partial support for these predictions. While anger responses tended to be consistent across conditions, the predicted anger-disgust contrasting outcome as a function of focus was not consistently observed. Interestingly, sadness was a recurrent response in the victim focus condition, suggesting that empathy-related emotions may co-occur with moral condemnation. Overall, the findings suggest that third-party moral appraisals reflect a broader emotional range that combines anger, disgust, but also sadness.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Sadness; Social psychology; Anger; Distancing; Moral disengagement; Focus; Moral development; Social cognitive theory of morality; Moral character
articles.category: Emotion, Facial Expression, Face, Social Cognition
articles.summary: 本研究通过两项预注册实验，测试了关注道德违规的受害者或施害者是否会引发不同情绪反应。参与者快速判断面部表情所表达的情绪是否与违规行为一致。结果显示愤怒反应在不同条件下一致，但未观察到预期的愤怒-厌恶对比；在受害者焦点条件下，悲伤是常见反应，表明共情情绪可能与道德谴责共存，揭示了第三方道德评价涉及更广泛的情绪范围。

articles.title: Beyond homophily: AI-driven analysis of facial and personality similarity preferences in online dating
articles.authors: Soonjae Kwon, Junkyu Jang, Sung-Hyuk Park
articles.abstract: Online dating platforms increasingly mediate human connection, yet the mechanisms by which users evaluate subjective traits like appearance and personality remain underexplored. To investigate these preference dynamics, this study applies AI-driven methods to a dataset of 506,014 interactions from 41,441 users on a major heterosexual dating platform in South Korea. By employing computer vision and large language models to quantify facial and personality similarity, our mixed-effects analysis reveals a significant gender asymmetry: women prefer facial similarity (homophily), whereas men exhibit a preference for facial dissimilarity (heterophily). Furthermore, personality preferences are found to be context-dependent; a partner's socioeconomic status moderates the demand for similarity, amplifying the preference for women while attenuating it for men in upward evaluation contexts. These findings offer actionable implications for the design of matching systems, suggesting that preferences in digitally mediated environments function as adaptive strategies shaped by gender and contextual cues.
articles.keywords: Personality; Psychology; Preference; Similarity; Big Five personality traits; Matching; Socioeconomic status; Social psychology
articles.summary: 本研究应用人工智能方法分析了韩国一个大型异性恋约会平台的数据集，发现性别不对称的偏好：女性倾向于选择面部相似的伴侣，而男性倾向于选择面部差异的伴侣。此外，个性相似性的偏好受社会经济地位调节，这为在线匹配系统的设计提供了实际建议。

articles.title: From bodily to digital co-presence: Unpacking the Interaction Ritual Chains of hashtag-driven group formation
articles.authors: Jinyan Duan, Siyu Qi, Hongyu Yan, Chong Wu
articles.abstract: This research integrates Interaction Ritual Chain (IRC) theory and Social Identity Theory (SIT) to construct a "Three-Stage Evolutionary Model," explaining how individuals coalesce into cohesive "ephemeral groups" around Douyin hashtags. Analyzing 80 trending topics through 5594 observations, the study reveals that in the Structural Assembly stage, hashtag popularity creates "digital co-presence," with dialogic interaction mediating 84.8 % of the effect by "stitching" traffic into a network structure. In the Symbolic Focusing stage, creative templates serve as "cognitive shortcuts" that drive linguistic convergence and opinion concentration, standardizing group consensus. Finally, in the Affective Forging stage, high emotional arousal suppresses rational dialogue while reinforcing in-group identity; furthermore, topic controversiality acts as a vital boundary condition for deep psychological synchronization and "defensive solidarity". By redefining "co-presence" for the algorithmic age, this study provides a systematic dynamical explanation for digital group cohesion.
articles.keywords: Dialogic; Social identity theory; Collective identity; Unpacking; Psychology; Identity; Social psychology; Cognitive science; Symbolic interactionism; Group; Construct
articles.category: Emotion, Affect, Social Cognition, Interpersonal Relationship
articles.summary: 本研究整合互动仪式链理论和社会认同理论，构建三阶段演化模型，解释个体如何围绕社交媒体标签形成数字群体。通过对抖音80个热门话题的5594次观察分析，发现在情感锻造阶段，高情绪唤醒抑制理性对话并强化群体内认同，而话题争议性作为边界条件促进了心理同步和防御性团结。

articles.title: Are your defense mechanisms harming you? The role of psychological defenses in social media comparison and adolescent self-esteem
articles.authors: Lin Ting Jin, Yun Jung Choi
articles.abstract: Adolescent experiences with social media are often depicted as "games of comparison." They are persistently aware of the gap between themselves and others. Such endless comparisons can profoundly affect self-esteem and trigger defense mechanisms. This study differentiates two modes of social comparison—ability comparison and opinion comparison—to examine the relationship between self-protective defense and self-expansive defense. By distinguishing between these two modes of social comparison and examining the roles of self-protective and self-expansive defense, the present study reveals the mediating role of psychological defense mechanisms on the effect of social comparison on self-esteem. Our study examines how adolescents use expressive interactions on social media as moderating variables to examine the efficacy of psychological defense mechanisms. The results show that self-protective defenses are not protective of self-esteem; they are detrimental, lowering self-esteem. Comparatively, self-expansive defenses enhance the mediating effect of self-esteem. In terms of social comparison, expressive interactive use can effectively promote self-expansive defense mechanisms, thereby improving self-esteem. This study revisits the meaning of defenses and highlights that the core value of defenses for adolescents lies not in how to defend against the pain of social comparison, but in the ability to reconstruct the self from imperfections while gaining insight into growth opportunities—a key pathway to psychological maturity and health.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Social psychology; Social media; Maturity; Meaning; Affect; Value; Prosocial behavior; Mechanism
articles.summary: 本研究探讨社交媒体中的社会比较对青少年自尊的影响，区分能力比较和意见比较，考察自我保护性防御和自我扩张性防御的中介作用。结果发现，自我保护性防御会降低自尊，而自我扩张性防御能增强自尊；表达性互动能通过促进自我扩张性防御来改善自尊。

articles.title: Personal cancer worry and Systemic Cancer Concern: Pathways to health behaviors via social media and emotional well-being
articles.authors: Chi-Chin Hsiao, Hsuan-Wei Lee
articles.abstract: Cancer worry and concern are associated with how individuals engage with and interpret digital health environments, yet existing research has not distinguished between personal worry about individual risk and systemic concerns regarding institutional responses. This study examines how personal worry and systemic cancer concern relate to digital health behaviors through distinct affective and trust-related pathways. Drawing on an integrated theoretical framework, we analyze nationally representative U.S. data (HINTS 6; N = 6252) using structural equation modeling. Personal cancer worry is associated with increased social media engagement and reduced emotional well-being, with emotional vulnerability in particular aligning with greater involvement in harmful behaviors (e.g., smoking), whereas social media engagement shows more selective behavioral associations. In contrast, systemic cancer concern, marked by institutional distrust, corresponds to limited or passive digital engagement and diminished emotional well-being, patterns that relate to lower participation in positive health behaviors. Social media engagement is associated with perceiving less health misinformation, showing modest behavioral associations. Overall, the findings distinguish two forms of cancer-related psychological responses: a pattern of active but emotionally vulnerable engagement associated with personal worry, and a pattern of trust-sensitive, low-engagement interpretation associated with systemic concern. Findings inform psychologically responsive digital health design. Emotion-sensitive features may support users with heightened vulnerability, whereas credibility cues may address institutional distrust. • Distinguishes Personal Cancer Worry (individual risk) from Systematic Cancer Worry (institutional distrust), revealing two distinct pathways influencing digital health behaviors. • Uses structural equation modeling (N=4245) showing personal worry increases social media use but reduces well-being, while systemic worry operates through emotional disruption. • Higher social media engagement correlates with lower perceived health misinformation, suggesting overconfidence among frequent users with digital literacy implications. • Provides recommendations for worry-aware platforms, including emotion-responsive interfaces for anxious users and trust-building features for skeptical populations.
articles.keywords: Worry; Psychology; Social media; Structural equation modeling; Credibility; Social psychology; Clinical psychology; Health communication; Digital media; Cancer
articles.summary: 本研究区分了个人癌症担忧和系统性癌症关注，探讨它们如何通过不同的情感和信任途径影响数字健康行为。基于结构方程模型对美国全国代表性数据的分析发现，个人担忧增加社交媒体参与但降低情绪福祉，且情感脆弱性与有害行为相关；而系统性关注与制度不信任相关，导致被动参与和更低情绪福祉。研究还发现社交媒体参与与较低感知健康错误信息相关，提示频繁用户可能存在过度自信。

articles.title: Exploring the association of social media connectivity and interactivity with ethnic polarization: An interdependent hierarchical affordance perspective
articles.authors: Qurban Hussain Pamirzad, Qiang Chen
articles.abstract: While research on social media polarization has surged in recent years, few studies have explored this phenomenon through an affordance perspective, particularly in ethnically divided societies. This study examined the relationship between social media affordance and ethnic polarization, utilizing cross-sectional survey data (N = 589) collected across 13 provinces in Afghanistan. Drawing on the affordance interdependence hierarchy guided by logical precedence, we found that low-level affordances - connectivity and interactivity - were positively associated with high-level affordances - plurality and collectivity. Additionally, connectivity was negatively associated with ethnic polarization, while interactivity showed no connection to it. Regarding the high-level affordances, both plurality and collectivity demonstrated positive associations with ethnic polarization, with plurality positively mediating the relationship between connectivity affordance and ethnic polarization. Examining the association of social media affordances with ethnic polarization in non-Western contexts expands the literature on social media polarization, yet necessitates further exploration of this dynamic in diverse settings.
articles.keywords: Affordance; Interactivity; Ethnic group; Social media; Psychology; Interdependence; Social psychology; Perspective; Polarization
articles.summary: 本研究基于可供性互赖层次理论，探讨了社会媒体连接性和互动性与民族两极化的关系。通过分析阿富汗的横断面调查数据发现，连接性与民族两极化呈负相关，而互动性则无直接影响；但多元性等高层次可供性与民族两极化正相关，其中多元性在连接性与民族两极化之间起中介作用。

articles.title: Unraveling the behavioral and autonomic dynamics of defensive states in humans in an unconstrained, immersive virtual world
articles.authors: Alma-Sophia Merscher, Lea K. Hildebrandt, Jasin Sahraoui, Matthias Gamer
articles.abstract: Defensive responses to threats are crucial for mental and physical health. Animal studies highlight different defense modes related to threat proximity, yet human defense dynamics remain underexplored due to experimental restrictions and oversimplification of research designs. The current multimodal study thus comprehensively characterized defense patterns in humans on the behavioral (movement speed, body, head, and eye rotations), autonomic (heart rate and skin conductance), and subjective level (perceived dread) in an immersive virtual reality environment. Ninety-four participants performed a foraging task while the context signaled varying levels of threat, that were also reflected in individual dread ratings. Upon relatively distal, ambiguous threat, and (oculo)motor activity decreased, reminiscent of freezing behaviors in rodents. While skin conductance levels increased when under attack, heart rate remained stable. Positive cross-correlations among movement metrics and autonomic measures as well as significant relationships of biobehavioral adjustments to successful coping behavior suggest the existence of circumscribed, adaptive defensive states that were further shaped by individual differences in anxiety and aggression. Overall, the current study offers valuable insights into the dynamic and integrated nature of defensive states in humans, while also establishing an ecologically valid research framework to advance translational research on threat processing across species.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Virtual world; Dynamics; Cognitive psychology; Virtual reality; Cognitive science; Human-computer interaction; Metaverse
articles.summary: 本研究利用沉浸式虚拟现实环境，综合考察了人类对威胁的防御反应，涵盖运动行为、自主神经活动和主观恐惧体验。结果发现，防御状态呈现动态适应性，各层面指标相互关联，并受焦虑和攻击性个体差异影响。

articles.title: We are nature: Engaging with community and nature to promote well-being and address environmental challenges.
articles.authors: Michela Lenzi, Brian D Christens
articles.keywords: Psychology; Public relations; Engineering ethics; Environmental planning; Community participation; Environmental ethics; Community engagement; Social psychology; MEDLINE

articles.title: You're not so bad after all: Apologies and guilt promote perceptions of moral restoration after wrongdoing
articles.authors: Emily P. Bosche, Justin M. Ludwig, Karina Schumann
articles.publish_info: Journal of Experimental Social Psychology, Vol. 124, 2026-05
articles.abstract: Interpersonal offenses can negatively affect perceptions of a wrongdoer's moral image. People often view wrongdoers as having experienced a lapse in moral judgment and may expect their future moral behavior to be compromised. What can wrongdoers do to restore their moral image in the eyes of others after committing an offense? In the current research, we examined whether wrongdoers who apologize or feel guilty are judged as more likely to show moral restoration in the future. Across five experiments that used hypothetical and recalled offenses, we found that wrongdoers who apologized or felt guilty were perceived as more moral in the future than those who offered no response. These effects were mediated by observers' perceptions that the wrongdoer shared their moral values and was committed to self-improvement. Furthermore, apologies and internal reflections that conveyed private remorse and responsibility were particularly effective in promoting the belief that the wrongdoer and victim should reconcile. This work demonstrates that apologies play an important role in fostering perceptions of moral restoration in the aftermath of an offense. • Tested whether apologies and guilt attenuate slippery slope perceptions. • Found two indirect paths between transgressor response and future moral evaluation. • Tested how transgressor responses impact third-party reconciliation endorsement.
articles.keywords: Wrongdoing; Psychology; Remorse; Social psychology; Morality; Forgiveness; Perception; Moral disengagement; Interpersonal communication; Affect
articles.summary: 本研究通过五项实验发现，在人际冒犯后，道歉和内疚感能够显著提升他人对冒犯者未来道德恢复的感知。这种效应由观察者认为冒犯者共享其道德价值观并承诺自我改进所中介。传达私人懊悔和责任的道歉与内省尤其能有效促进冒犯者与受害者应和解的信念。

articles.title: Toward a predictive model of moral concern
articles.authors: Bastian Jaeger, Matti Wilks, Caspar van Lissa
articles.publish_info: Journal of Experimental Social Psychology, Vol. 124, 2026-05
articles.abstract: At the heart of many contentious debates (e.g., on abortion, immigration, or meat consumption, is the question of how much we ought to weigh the welfare and interests of different entities. Previous research has identified numerous characteristics that predict how much concern people show toward different entities, such as empathy or perceived similarity. However, many of these characteristics are correlated, making it difficult to disentangle their unique relation to moral concern, and there is little evidence on the relative importance of different characteristics. We aim to address these issues and move the field toward an integrative model of moral concern. We reviewed the literature to identify hypothesized predictors of moral concern. We will then use a machine learning approach to simultaneously test all identified predictors and build an integrative and parsimonious predictive model ( n = 800 U.S. participants). Our findings will provide insights into (1) how accurately we can predict moral concern with the characteristics that were identified in previous research, and (2) which characteristics are most important for predicting moral concern.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Empathy; Social psychology; Moral disengagement; Defining Issues Test; Field; Social cognitive theory of morality; Test; Morality; Relation
articles.summary: 本研究旨在构建道德关注的预测模型，整合共情、感知相似性等特征，采用机器学习方法对800名美国参与者进行测试，以评估预测准确性并识别关键预测因子。

articles.title: Dominance through the lens of a competitive worldview: The role of relationship expectancies
articles.authors: Dean Baltiansky, Daniel R. Ames
articles.publish_info: Journal of Experimental Social Psychology, Vol. 124, 2026-05
articles.abstract: Who behaves dominantly—and why? Much compelling prior research spotlights motivational sources. We focus here on beliefs, proposing that people are less likely to behave dominantly when they expect dominance to incur greater relationship costs. We posit that this situation-specific expectancy is shaped by a general competitive worldview, seeing the social world as a "competitive jungle." In five preregistered studies, we tested whether those with a competitive worldview expected dominance to incur less relationship harm and whether expected relationship harm predicted dominance. In Study 1 ( N = 275), part- and full-time workers completed widely used scales of dominance and worldviews, allowing us to test our predicted effects and alternatives. Study 2 ( N = 289) shifted from scales to employee-recounted acts of real-world managerial dominance. Studies 3A and 3B ( N = 1192) featured a novel paradigm for capturing behavioral dominance, with participants role-playing as managers and employees in an incentive-compatible design. Study 4 ( N = 492) manipulated expectancies to test their impact on behavior. We found support for our predictions across our studies, showing that a competitive worldview shaped relationship expectancies and that those who expected less relational damage from dominance were more likely to endorse or enact dominant behavior. These results emerged controlling for various motivational measures (e.g., relationship concern) and other expectancies (e.g., expected compliance), supporting a belief-based account of dominance that complements past work on motivational sources. Exploratory analyses suggested that those behaving dominantly may underestimate relational harm whereas those eschewing dominance may sometimes overestimate it.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Dominance; Expectancy theory; Social psychology; Harm; Social dominance orientation; Exploratory research; Test; Social relation
articles.summary: 本研究探讨了支配行为的信念基础，提出竞争世界观通过塑造对支配行为关系成本的预期来影响支配行为。五项预注册研究发现，预期支配会带来较少关系损害的人更可能表现出支配行为，这补充了以往基于动机的研究。

articles.title: Proximity to whiteness and the racial position of multiracial people in the United States
articles.authors: A. Chyei Vinluan, Maria G. Garay, Jennifer M. Perry, Linda X. Zou, Keith B. Maddox, Jessica D. Remedios
articles.publish_info: Journal of Experimental Social Psychology, Vol. 124, 2026-05
articles.abstract: In the United States, Multiracial people with White ancestry may benefit from racial privilege not accessible to their monoracial minority counterparts. We examined this possibility by studying the racial position (Zou & Cheryan, 2017) of Multiracial-White groups (Study 1) and individuals (Study 2). In Study 1, Multiracial-White (specifically, Asian-White, Black-White, and Latino-White) groups were perceived as superior compared to their monoracial minority counterparts, and Asian-White and Latino-White groups were perceived as more American than monoracial Asian and Latino groups. In Study 2, racially ambiguous individuals were rated as higher in superiority and Americanness when they were described as having one White parent versus two minority parents. Furthermore, we examined perceptions of White phenotypicality as a possible mechanism underlying stereotypes that privilege Multiracial-White people. Indeed, racially ambiguous individuals were perceived as looking more phenotypically White when they were described as having one White parent versus two minority parents. Judgments of White phenotypicality partially mediated higher ratings of superiority and Americanness ascribed to individuals who identified as Multiracial-White (versus as monoracial minority). In sum, the present studies shed light on the mechanisms that may convey and sustain White privilege to Multiracial-White people.
articles.keywords: Gender studies; Position; Social psychology; Psychology; Race; Ethnic group; Sociology; Racism; Racial formation theory; Perception
articles.summary: 本研究通过两项实验考察了美国多种族人的种族地位，发现具有白人血贯的多种族群体和个体在优越性和美国性方面被感知为更高，且白人表型感知部分中介了这种效应。这揭示了种族特权如何在多种族人中被维持和传递的机制。

articles.title: "If you agree with me, it must be true": Social verification creates shared reality and consolidates impressions
articles.authors: Matteo Masi, Gerrit Lamers, Gerald Echterhoff
articles.publish_info: Journal of Experimental Social Psychology, Vol. 124, 2026-05
articles.abstract: A scarcely studied route to shared reality is the social verification of one's impressions, a process that should promote impression consolidation. We tested this prediction across four experiments ( N = 1342) using a hiring-decision paradigm. Participants evaluated pairs of similarly qualified applicants based on brief descriptions and portrait photos, made hiring decisions alongside a fictitious partner, and received either verification or no verification of their choices. Impression consolidation was assessed via subsequent speeded trait attributions. We expected stronger consolidation following verification, reflected in greater evaluative choice-consistency (i.e., attributing more positive traits to chosen than unchosen applicants), indexed by signal detection theory's discriminability, as well as increased accessibility (response latencies). Participants also reported experienced shared reality with the partner. Across studies, social verification reliably increased experienced shared reality and choice-consistent discriminability. Accessibility effects, in contrast, were inconsistent and context-dependent. Experiment 3 showed that verification enhanced choice-consistent discriminability only for recruitment-relevant traits. Experiment 4 showed no trait-specific consolidation for explicit, implied, or unrelated profile traits beyond evaluative choice-consistency. Moreover, across studies, experienced shared reality was positively associated with choice-consistent discriminability, but not with accessibility. Together, these findings indicate that social verification selectively consolidates impressions by stabilizing their evaluative structure—particularly on relevant domains—rather than by uniformly enhancing accessibility. Therefore, our results showed that even brief verification from an unfamiliar partner can strengthen shared reality and reinforce impressions primarily through increased evaluative consistency. Implications for impression formation and motivated cognition are discussed. • Shared reality (SR) reduces uncertainty about one's judgments and impressions. • We tested whether social verification boosts SR and strengthens impressions. • In a collaborative hiring task, SR increased when peers verified decisions. • Verification increased evaluative choice-consistency in trait attributions. • Social verification and SR can shape social cognition and judgments.
articles.keywords: Impression formation; Psychology; Impression; Social psychology; Consolidation; Social reality; Trait; Social perception; Impression management; Precedent
articles.summary: 本研究通过招聘决策范式探讨了社会验证如何创造共享现实并巩固印象。实验发现，社会验证显著增强了参与者的共享现实体验，并提升了选择一致性的特质归因区分度，但对反应时可及性的影响不一致。结果表明，社会验证主要通过稳定评估结构来选择性巩固印象，而非均匀增强可及性。

articles.title: Ensemble perception in entitativity judgments of natural crowds
articles.authors: Sarah Ariel Lamer, Spencer Dobbs, Lindsay Goolsby, Timothy D. Sweeny, Max Weisbuch
articles.publish_info: Journal of Experimental Social Psychology, Vol. 124, 2026-05
articles.abstract: We conducted two studies to examine how rapid ensemble perceptions of emotion shape judgments of entitativity. We hypothesize that when a perceiver encounters a crowd, they rapidly discern the degree of emotional variability in that group via ensemble coding, and this input provides a strong basis for both immediate and deliberative entitativity inferences. We test this hypothesis using images of a representative sample of natural groups collected via Instagram®. Each person in each of ∼200 group images was independently coded for facial affect, gender, and race. Participants evaluated facial emotion variability in each group. We hypothesized that people perceive group variability in facial emotion after brief exposure (150 ms), and that these rapid perceptions predict entitativity judgments of groups viewed for longer periods (3 s). We further hypothesized that rapid ensemble perceptions of emotion variability support the rapid formation of entitativity judgments: entitativity judgments made after 150 ms will approximate those made after 3 s. In Study 1, participants saw images for 5 s each, evaluating (a) shared emotion among group members or (b) entitativity. Study 2 employed a 3 (exposure: 150 ms, 750 ms, 3 s) x 2 (rating: shared emotion, entitativity) x 2 (faces visible: yes, no) between-subjects design. All hypotheses were supported, and the effects were largely independent of the race and gender composition of the groups. These data suggest that complex conceptual judgments about small groups can be formed in the initial instants of perception. We discuss the implications for ensemble coding theories and methods, as well as for entitativity theories.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Social psychology; Perception; Social perception; Emotion perception; Face perception; Developmental psychology; Cognitive psychology; Social relation; Coding; Facial expression
articles.matched_keywords: emotion, affect, facial expression, emotion perception
articles.category: Emotion, Facial Expression, Emotion Perception, Face, Social Cognition
articles.summary: 本研究通过两个实验探讨了快速面部情绪集合感知如何影响人们对群体实体性的判断。结果发现，即使在短暂暴露下，观察者也能感知群体情绪变异性，并基于此形成实体性判断，且这些早期判断与较长时间观看后的判断高度一致。该效应独立于群体的种族和性别构成。

articles.title: Principles of nostalgia: Meta-analytic tests
articles.authors: Evan Weingarten, Ziwei Wei, Tim Wildschut, Constantine Sedikides
articles.publish_info: Journal of Experimental Social Psychology, Vol. 124, 2026-05
articles.abstract: Nostalgia, a sentimental longing for one's meaningful past, has a long and turbulent history. Once regarded a medical disease, psychiatric disorder, and clinical ailment, the emotion is now recognized to be frequent, positive, and occurring across ages. But when is nostalgia elicited and what is it for? Guided by relevant literature, and relying on experimental evidence, we derived five principles: Threat triggers nostalgia (Principle I); Nostalgia confers psychological benefits: dynamism, existential, social, consumption, morality, well-being (Principle II); Nostalgia's benefits generalize across cultures (Principle III); Nostalgia's benefits are contingent on its Person × Activity fit (Principle IV); Nostalgia is more than positivity (Principle V). We tested these principles meta-analytically using two datasets: (a) the Triggers dataset containing 102 effect sizes from 60 experiments reported in 34 documents (g = 0.428, 95% CI [0.361, 0.495]) in regard to Principle I, and (b) the Benefits dataset containing 1460 effect sizes from 347 experiments reported in 167 documents (g = 0.388, 95% CI [0.355, 0.420]) in regard to Principles II-V. The meta-analytic results were consistent with all five principles. Selection bias methods indicated a modest downward adjustment of the aggregate effect sizes. The findings contribute toward establishing nostalgia as a fundamental human emotion, relevant not only to psychology but also to allied disciplines.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Selection; Social psychology; Social perception; Cognitive psychology; Experimental psychology; Aggregate; Epistemology; Impression formation
articles.summary: 本研究通过元分析检验了怀旧情绪的五个原则，包括威胁触发怀旧、怀旧带来多种心理益处、益处跨文化泛化等。基于两个大规模数据集的元分析结果支持了所有原则，表明怀旧是一种基本人类情绪，对心理学及相关学科具有重要意义。

articles.title: Income inequality and status concerns in multiple life domains increase self-objectification for both women and men
articles.authors: K. Blake, M. Kowal, B. Bastian, J. Kuek, A. Harrington, S. Harmon-Jones, R.C. Brooks
articles.publish_info: Journal of Experimental Social Psychology, Vol. 124, 2026-05
articles.abstract: Self-objectification—viewing one's worth through physical appearance—has detrimental effects. It is typically explained by heteronormative conditions and female sexual objectification, but we propose that it can also stem from economic conditions and status concerns. Experiment 1 manipulated economic inequality and shows that it increases self-objectification directly and indirectly through status anxiety in multiple life domains. These domains were reducible to appearance, parenting, material, and life achievement status anxieties; all except parenting robustly increased self-objectification. Experiment 2 manipulated economic inequality and status anxiety in three domains (career success, appearance success, control). Both manipulations predicted self-objectification but did not interact, indicating an independent path between status concerns and self-objectification. While career success reduced self-objectification, appearance success reinforced rather than alleviated it. Appearance success also reduced some socioeconomic status anxieties but increased educational status concerns, indicating that appearance behaviors like self-objectification can provide a sense of status in multiple areas of life, despite being non-optimal overall. Across both experiments, effects were robust to controls and vastly few sex or gender differences emerged. Locating self-objectification within broader status-seeking processes clarifies its persistence even as patriarchal norms lessen and reveals that these processes are only weakly gendered, challenging prevailing theories and highlighting new avenues for intervention.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Economic inequality; Social psychology; Inequality; Social inequality; Demographic economics; Socioeconomic status; Affect; Life course approach
articles.summary: 本研究探讨经济不平等和社会地位关注如何增加自我物化。通过实验操纵发现，地位焦虑直接且间接地促进自我物化，且效应在男性和女性中普遍存在，表明自我物化是更广泛地位寻求过程的一部分，挑战了传统理论。

articles.title: Emotional resonance and cognitive processing in cross-media art: A psychological analysis of the synthesis of photography and painting in the works of Liu Xiaodong.
articles.authors: Lin Yang
articles.journal: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts
articles.publish_info: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts, 2026-05-11
articles.abstract: Works that combine photography and painting present novel challenges for perception. This study identifies the psychological mechanisms underlying emotional resonance and cognitive processing in the perception of Liu Xiaodong's photo-painting synthesis. A comprehensive methodological approach was employed, including 64-channel electroencephalogram recordings, eye tracking, physiological responses, behavioral assessments, and semistructured interviews. The experimental sample consisted of 120 participants: 60 experts (with more than 5 years of formal art education) and 60 novices (with fewer than two gallery visits per year). Participants viewed 12 works by Liu Xiaodong alongside control images under varying experimental conditions (2 × 2 × 3 factorial design). Eye-tracking analysis indicated enhanced information processing: Gaze duration on faces averaged 6,234 ms, whereas transition probability between regions rose to .47 (compared with .34 for photographs and .28 for paintings). Statistical analysis identified four principal components of responses (explaining 87.5% of variance): empathy (34.2%), cognitive effort (22.8%), aesthetic experience (18.4%), and existential reflection (12.1%). The findings inform the design of art-therapy protocols for trauma rehabilitation, supporting a two-phase activation model (initial phase = 3.8 µs, integrative phase = 4.9 µs). For curatorial practice, a minimum viewing duration of 6.2 s is recommended. Presenting guiding questions before viewing enhanced engagement by 38%-57%. Educational programs should foster the ability to discern the dual aspects of photographic documentary realism and the transformative power of painting. Overall, the study advances theories of embodied art perception by documenting physiological responses that correspond to the thematic content of the artworks.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Painting; Cognition; Psychological analysis; Photography; Cognitive psychology; Cognitive science; Information processing
articles.summary: 本研究考察了刘小东摄影绘画合成作品中情绪共鸣与认知处理的心理机制。通过脑电图、眼动追踪等多模态测量，发现观看时注视时间延长，区域转移概率增加，并提取出共情、认知努力等四个响应主成分。研究提出了艺术感知的两阶段激活模型，为艺术疗法和策展实践提供了指导。

articles.title: The Persian Author Recognition Test: Exposure to literary and popular fiction differently predicts theory of mind.
articles.authors: Danyal Tohidi, Emanuele Castano
articles.journal: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts
articles.publish_info: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts, 2026-05-11
articles.abstract: Storytelling has long been hailed as a critical factor in human evolution, notably facilitating group living by fostering the development of advanced social cognition. Consistently, research in Western cultures has shown that exposure to literary, but not popular, fiction, measured via the Author Recognition Test, predicts performance on advanced theory of mind (ToM) measures. Here we report results from 2 studies that aimed to validate the Persian Author Recognition Test and to examine the differential effects of exposure to literary versus popular fiction on ToM in Iran. Across these studies ( n = 449 and 454), factor analyses confirmed a 2-factor structure distinguishing recognition of literary and popular fiction authors, and multiple regression analyses showed that exposure to literary, but not popular, fiction positively predicted performance on a ToM task. Exposure to literary fiction also positively predicted scores on a self-reported ToM measure, but this was significant only in Study 1. These effects emerged controlling for personality traits and demographic variables. Gender differences favoring females on ToM measures were also observed. These findings extend the link between exposure to literary fiction and ToM to the Persian cultural context and contribute to the growing body of evidence showing the differential effects of different types of fiction on social cognition.
articles.keywords: Persian; Psychology; Literature; Narrative; Linguistics; Social psychology; Popular fiction; Aesthetics; Popular culture
articles.summary: 本研究通过两项实验验证了波斯作者识别测试，并考察了文学小说与流行小说对心智理论的差异效应。结果显示，接触文学小说能正向预测心智理论表现，而流行小说则无此效应，且这一关联在控制人格特质和人口变量后依然存在。这些发现将文学小说促进社会认知的跨文化联系扩展到了波斯语境。

articles.title: The role of self-conscious emotions in everyday creativity: A daily diary study.
articles.authors: Andreea Sutu, Anna G. Snyder, Adam K. Fetterman, Rodica Ioana Damian
articles.journal: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts
articles.publish_info: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts, 2026-05-21
articles.abstract: Little research has investigated the link between self-conscious emotions and everyday creativity. In the current study ( N = 120), we used a daily diary design to investigate the association between self-conscious emotions and several measures of everyday creativity (everyday creative behaviors, depth and breadth of everyday creative behaviors, and self-perceived everyday creativity). Participants completed a baseline questionnaire and online daily diaries assessing self-conscious emotions (i.e., authentic and hubristic pride, shame, and guilt) and creativity for 14 days. The results indicated that daily feelings of authentic pride were positively associated with self-reported everyday creative behaviors, breadth of everyday creative behaviors, and self-perceived everyday creativity. Daily feelings of hubristic pride were positively associated with self-reported everyday creative behaviors and the depth of everyday creative behaviors. Daily feelings of shame were negatively associated with self-perceived everyday creativity, and daily feelings of guilt were not found to be statistically significantly associated with any everyday creativity outcomes. Exploratory analyses indicated that authentic pride predicted everyday creativity outcomes above and beyond daily positive and negative affect, though the remaining self-conscious emotions did not demonstrate meaningful incremental predictive validity. The findings contribute to functional accounts of emotions and substantiate that self-conscious emotions—particularly authentic pride—are associated with everyday creativity.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Social psychology; Cognitive psychology; Developmental psychology; Everyday life; Affect; Perception; Applied psychology; Peer review
articles.summary: 本研究采用日记法探讨自我意识情绪（如真实骄傲、傲慢骄傲、羞耻、内疚）与日常创造力的每日关联。结果发现，真实骄傲与创造性行为、行为广度及自我感知创造力正相关，羞耻与自我感知创造力负相关，而内疚无显著影响；真实骄傲的预测效应独立于日常积极和消极情感状态。

articles.title: The psychological impact of visual art in the workplace on employees: A systematic mixed-studies review.
articles.authors: Mick Cooper, Maritina Peristeri
articles.journal: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts
articles.publish_info: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts, 2026-05-18
articles.abstract: Workplaces can be significant sources of psychological distress. Hence, it is imperative to find means of promoting psychological health in this context. Visual art is of proven capacity to improve mental well-being. In this systematic mixed-studies review, we evaluated whether visual art in the workplace—compared with no art or a standard workplace—could improve psychological outcomes and, if so, in what ways. Eligible studies provided empirical data on the impact of visual art on employees. We searched Web of Science, APA PsycInfo, and Google Scholar (September 2-25, 2024), with risk of bias assessed using the Mixed Methods Appraisal Tool. Thematic analysis combined qualitative and quantitative findings. Of the 909 articles identified, we selected 24 for full-text review, and reached a final selection of eight articles, inclusive of 10 studies and approximately 1,220 participants. Three of the studies were analogue randomized controlled trials, one was a quasi-experimental study, and the remainder were quantitative or qualitative surveys or interviews. Quality assessment was high for two interview studies, but otherwise moderate or low. We identified four principal domains of impact: (a) psychological well-being : improved physical and psychological comfort, increased psychological stimulation; (b) psychological distress : significant reductions in stress and anger for males; (c) interpersonal outcomes : greater social interactions; and (d) work-related outcomes : greater efficiency and satisfaction. Our findings indicate that the positive psychological effects of viewing visual art extend to the workplace. However, there is a need to establish mechanisms of action, and moderators, within this context.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Applied psychology; Cognitive psychology; Social psychology; Creativity; Visual methods; Perception; Visual perception
articles.summary: 本系统综述评估了工作场所视觉艺术对员工心理的影响，发现艺术能改善心理舒适度、减少压力（尤其是男性的压力和愤怒）、增加社交互动并提高工作效率和满意度。研究基于综合定性和定量数据，但指出需要进一步探索作用机制和调节因素。

articles.title: What moves the museum? Evidence on indoor spatial and design principles shaping affective and aesthetic responses.
articles.authors: Marta Pizzolante, Caterina Restano, Andrea Gaggioli
articles.journal: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts
articles.publish_info: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts, 2026-05-18
articles.abstract: The design of space plays a crucial role in shaping human perception, emotion, and cognition, particularly in cultural settings such as museums. However, systematic evidence on how specific indoor spatial and design features of museums influence affective and aesthetic responses remains limited, largely due to methodological constraints. This study examined the effects of four visual design principles (VDPs)-color, lighting, balance, and form dynamism-on affective states and aesthetic evaluations within a virtual indoor museum environment. To this end, eight virtual reality environments depicting the same museum space were developed, each manipulating one VDP at two opposing levels. Forty-seven participants (35 women, 12 men; M age = 25.72, SD age = 7.52) explored all virtual reality environments in a fully counterbalanced within-subjects design and completed self-report measures of affective (pleasure, arousal, and dominance) and aesthetic responses following exposure to each environment. Cumulative link mixed models revealed significant effects of color, balance, and form dynamism on both affective and aesthetic outcomes. Warm-color palettes, symmetrical layouts, and curvilinear forms were consistently associated with higher ratings of pleasure and dominance, as well as aesthetic attraction and perceived immersion. In contrast, arousal did not vary significantly across conditions, and lighting manipulations did not produce reliable effects on any affective or aesthetic measure. These findings indicate that specific VDPs are selectively related to more positive affective states and aesthetic evaluations, in immersive virtual spatial contexts. From an applied perspective, the results provide preliminary evidence informing the design of museum-like environments, offering empirically grounded guidelines for optimizing visitor experience in cultural settings.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Cognitive psychology; Social psychology; Perception; Design elements and principles; Aesthetics; Affect; Cognitive science; Creativity
articles.summary: 本研究考察了博物馆室内空间的设计原则（颜色、照明、平衡和形式动态性）对情感状态和美学评价的影响。通过虚拟现实实验，发现温暖色调、对称布局和曲线形式能显著提升愉悦感和支配感，并增强美学吸引力和沉浸感；而唤醒状态和照明效果在不同条件下无显著变化。

articles.title: Review of The creativity choice: The science of making decisions to turn ideas into actions.
articles.authors: Jeffrey K. Smith
articles.journal: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts
articles.publish_info: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts, 2026-05-14
articles.keywords: Creativity; Psychology; Epistemology; Engineering ethics; Cognitive science; Ideation; Psychological science; Social psychology; Making-of

articles.title: From myth to model: Representation of "the Jew" in generative AI.
articles.authors: Gal Gutman, Michael Gilead
articles.abstract: Antisemitism, a persistent form of prejudice, is increasingly manifesting in subtle and modernized forms within contemporary society. This study examined how cultural representations of Jews are encoded in large language models (LLMs), which absorb societal discourse through training on vast corpora of human-generated text. To investigate these representations, we developed an indirect methodology: generating character biographies using Jewish and non-Jewish names, removing overt identity markers, and prompting the LLM to evaluate a range of psychological and sociocultural traits. Characters associated with Jewish (vs. non-Jewish) names were consistently rated as more competent, privileged, dominant, self-controlled, future-oriented, hierarchical, and obsessive, and as less likable and less collectivistic. When these trait profiles were mapped onto fictional characters, they aligned with archetypes historically associated with antisemitic tropes. These findings demonstrate how latent antisemitic stereotypes can persist in AI systems despite explicit bias mitigation, highlighting the adaptability of prejudice and the structural embedding of cultural archetypes in contemporary technologies.
articles.keywords: Sociocultural evolution; Linguistics; Prejudice; Archetype; Representation; Psychology; Identity; Character; Generative grammar; Mythology
articles.summary: 本研究探讨大型语言模型中对犹太人的文化表征，通过生成角色传记并让模型评估心理特质，发现与犹太名字相关的人物被赋予更competent、dominant等特征，与历史反犹太主义刻板印象原型一致，揭示了AI系统中潜在偏见的持久性和文化原型的结构性嵌入。

articles.title: From praise to prejudice: How positive stereotypes reinforce antisemitism.
articles.authors: Bella Qing Dong, Margaret Shih, Todd Pittinsky
articles.abstract: Antisemitism-defined as prejudice and hostility toward Jews-is traditionally studied by examining explicit negative stereotypes and historical discriminatory actions against Jewish communities. However, this article explores an often overlooked and understudied dimension: how positive stereotypes about Jewish people paradoxically reinforce antisemitism and contribute to their marginalization. While negative stereotypes about Jews are widely recognized as harmful and unwelcomed, positive stereotypes-such as perception of high intelligence and financial achievements-are frequently misunderstood as benign. We investigate how these seemingly positive traits could facilitate and reinforce negative outcomes for Jewish people. We propose, analogous to the framework built on the model minority myth literature in Asian American Studies literature, that positive stereotypes also perpetuate antisemitism through three psychological mechanisms: (a) othering, social alienation, and eroded social solidarity; (b) fostering resentment and neglecting diversity of ingroup experiences; and (c) enabling scapegoating and conspiracy narratives. We explore their consequences at the individual, group, and societal levels.
articles.keywords: Praise; Psychology; Social psychology; Developmental psychology; Interpersonal relationship; Criticism; Perception; Qualitative research
articles.summary: 本研究探讨了积极刻板印象如何反常地强化反犹太主义。文章提出了三种心理机制，包括他者化、怨恨和替罪羊叙事，分析了这些机制在个人、群体和社会层面如何导致边缘化。

articles.title: Emulation versus envy: Jewish managerial stereotypes in China.
articles.authors: Joshua Keller, Wen Shan
articles.abstract: Does the perception of Jewish financial success trigger antisemitism? In three studies using both qualitative and quantitative methods, we found that the answer in China was no. In Study 1, we examined Chinese popular discourse on Jewish businesses and found that Jewish managers were seen as models to emulate for both instrumental success and ethical conduct. In Study 2, a survey of Chinese white-collar workers showed a similar pattern: Managerial behaviors perceived as Jewish were rated as more profitable and ethical than those perceived as more Chinese. In Study 3, through an investment-scenario experiment, Chinese participants rated businesses with managers described as adhering to Chinese views of Jewish managerial behavior as more profitable and ethical, and these evaluations were mediated by the extent to which participants perceived the behavior as "Jewish." These results offer insights into how cultural context shapes perceptions of Jewish financial success as either a source of envy or of emulation.
articles.keywords: Emulation; Psychology; Social psychology; Judaism; Applied psychology; Structural equation modeling; Sociology; Personality
articles.summary: 本研究通过三项实验考察了在中国背景下，对犹太金融成功的感知是否会引发反犹太主义。结果发现，中国公众倾向于将犹太经理视为在成功和道德上值得效仿的榜样，而非产生嫉妒情绪。研究揭示了文化背景对情绪感知和社会判断的塑造作用。

articles.title: Collective emotion regulation.
articles.authors: Amit Goldenberg, Yajun Cao
articles.abstract: Collective emotional responses represent responses of multiple people to an emotional situation. Such responses are ubiquitous in many contexts, such as conflicts, crises, and celebrations. However, a closer look at processes of collective emotions leads to an observation: Collective emotions often co-occurs with attempts to influence the emotions of the group. We refer to these attempts as collective emotion regulation, where members of groups aim to change collective emotions. In this article, we first define collective emotion and propose a model of collective emotion generation. We then define collective emotion regulation and distinguish it from related concepts such as interpersonal emotion regulation. We propose a process model to describe the unfolding of collective emotion regulation that helps organize a variety of behaviors previously discussed in other literatures. We further show how the concept can help explain collective behavior and design interventions aimed at regulating collective emotions. Finally, we discuss open empirical questions and future directions.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Cognitive psychology; Collective behavior; Collective memory; Value; Social psychology; Realization; Collective intelligence
articles.summary: 本研究定义了集体情绪调节的概念，将其与人际情绪调节等区分开来，并提出了一个过程模型来描述集体情绪调节的动态过程。该模型有助于解释集体行为并指导旨在调节集体情绪的干预措施设计。

articles.title: Ethical risk prevention in client generative artificial intelligence (genAI) use: Applying the primary risk-management model.
articles.authors: Gihun Im
articles.abstract: As clients increasingly use generative artificial intelligence for emotional support, psychologists now deal with unfamiliar ethical challenges that go beyond conventional risk-management strategies. While generative artificial intelligence systems like ChatGPT or therapy chatbots can be easily accessible, low cost, and oddly compassionate, using them without oversight introduces empirically documented risks such as misinformation and sycophancy, as well as emerging concerns related to cultural bias and potential therapeutic alliance disruption. Drawing from the American Psychological Association (2017) and ethical reflections offered by Chenneville et al. (2024), this conceptual and practice-oriented article applies Crowley and Gottlieb's (2012) primary risk-management model to build a forward-looking, preventive approach to these new challenges. The primary risk-management model emphasizes five iterative stages: resource accumulation, attention and detection of potential risk, initial appraisal of potential risk, preliminary risk-management efforts, and elicitation and use of feedback. Through conceptual mapping and application to a worst-case illustration, this article shows how primary risk-management model-based prevention can strengthen ethical alertness and reflexive judgment in psychologists facing generative artificial intelligence-related dilemmas. The discussion concludes with suggestions for education, professional policy, and empirical research, all aimed at reinforcing preparedness and reducing predictable harm.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Generative grammar; Reflexivity; Debriefing; Misinformation; Metacognition; Situational ethics; Deception; PsycINFO
articles.summary: 本研究针对客户使用生成式AI进行情感支持时出现的伦理风险，如虚假信息、文化偏见和治疗联盟破坏，应用主要风险管理模型提出预防策略。文章通过概念映射和案例分析，展示了如何增强心理学家在AI相关困境中的伦理意识和反思判断，并对教育、政策和研究提出建议。

articles.title: Multilevel associations between discrimination and sleep health among ethnically and racially minoritized students during the college transition: Coping as a mediator.
articles.authors: Tiffany Yip, Zhenqiang Zhao, Kyle Lorenzo, Enrique Dimagiba, Jinjin Yan, Nidia Ruedas-Gracia, Heining Cham, David Chae, Mona El Sheikh
articles.abstract: Ethnically and racially minoritized college students encounter various forms of discrimination, which disrupt sleep health. However, the mechanisms linking daily and chronic discrimination and sleep are less clear. This study investigated coping responses as a mediator linking discrimination and sleep at both the daily and chronic levels. The analytic sample includes 482 ethnically and racially minoritized first-year college students (Mage = 18.4 years, SD = 0.45; 77% female; 24% Asian or Asian American; 15% Black, African American or West Indian; 25% Hispanic or Latinx; 35% multiracial; and 1% other) at a predominantly White institution in the northeastern United States. Everyday discrimination, ethnic and racial discrimination, coping responses to stressful events, and sleep were measured with self-reported, 14-day daily diary surveys. Results from multilevel mediation analysis showed that rumination fully mediated the within person-level associations between both everyday discrimination and ethnic and racial discrimination and sleep onset latency. Rumination also mediated the between person-level associations between everyday discrimination and ethnic and racial discrimination and sleep health (i.e., sleep duration, sleep quality, respectively). Although discrimination was associated with problem solving, problem solving was not associated with sleep within or across participants. Discrimination is a daily and chronic social stressor that is associated with impaired sleep health. This study shows that this association is explained by ruminative arousal. Leveraging these novel findings to promote sleep health among ethnically and racially minoritized college students during the college transition, implications for theory, research, and practice are discussed.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Coping; Ethnic group; Stressor; Clinical psychology; Rumination; Sleep; Racism; Multilevel model; Mediation
articles.summary: 本研究探讨了种族和民族少数群体大学生在大学过渡期间，日常和慢性歧视与睡眠健康之间的关联，并以应对反应作为中介。通过14天日记调查和多层次中介分析，发现反刍完全中介了歧视与睡眠起始潜伏期、睡眠质量及持续时间的关联，而问题解决未显示中介作用。结果表明，歧视作为社会压力源，通过反刍唤醒损害睡眠健康。

articles.title: The impact of racial diversity within college campuses for students of color.
articles.authors: Chanel Meyers, Jenny Kim, Gretchen Nihill, Marcus Cumberbatch
articles.abstract: Student populations in higher education are rapidly increasing in diversity. Despite their access to higher education, students of color still face numerous structural and interpersonal barriers to their success within these institutions. Plethora of research suggests that more racially diverse groups improve climate and performance within institutions; however, it is less clear whether it matters where this diversity is represented. We experimentally tested whether racial diversity on college campuses would shape important student-related outcomes, such as sense of belonging, interests in the institution, and their perceptions of the institution's commitment to diversity. Importantly, we examine whether these effects of racial diversity differ when this representation is presented in student versus faculty populations on campus. Across two studies, we find that racially diverse campuses lead to more belonging, college interest, and honest perceptions of an institution's commitment to diversity, which are crucial for students to both enter and succeed within institutions of higher learning. However, we detect no differences whether this diversity is represented within students versus faculty. Critically, we find no differences in these effects among students of color and White students. These findings suggest that greater racial diversity on college campuses is essential to fostering positive student-related outcomes, not only for students of color but also for all students.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Diversity; Ethnic group; Cultural diversity; Social psychology; Racial diversity; Racial differences; Pedagogy; Racism; Racial bias
articles.summary: 本研究通过实验考察了大学校园种族多样性对学生相关结果的影响。研究发现，种族多样性能够增强学生的归属感、对机构的兴趣以及对机构多样性承诺的感知，但这些效应在学生与教职员工多样性之间没有显著差异，且不同种族学生间也无差异。

articles.title: Acculturation latent profiles as moderators of the relations between racial and gender barriers and outcome expectations among Latinx engineering undergraduates.
articles.authors: Jinkoo Lee, Lisa Y. Flores
articles.abstract: Latinx engineering undergraduate students often face racial and gender barriers that shape their career expectations. Guided by Berry's acculturation framework, this study tested whether latent cultural orientation profiles moderate these relationships. Participants were 821 Latinx engineering undergraduate students (48% women; Mage = 20.1, SD = 1.7). Latent profile analysis identified four profiles: bicultural, moderately bicultural, assimilated, and separated. A latent mixture regression model tested whether profiles moderated prospective associations between Time 1 barriers and Time 2 engineering outcome expectations, controlling for baseline expectations and gender. Profiles significantly moderated the effect of gender barriers, Wald χ²(3) = 10.25, p = .017. Only the assimilated group (Class 3) showed a significant positive slope (b = 0.27, p = .042). In contrast, other groups showed nonsignificant slopes. No overall moderation emerged for racial barriers, Wald χ²(3) = 6.91, p = .075, though the separated group (Class 4) showed a significant negative slope (b = -0.66, p = .048), indicating vulnerability to racial barriers. Findings highlight the importance of considering cultural orientation profiles when examining how barriers influence Latinx students' career development. Whereas gender barriers may serve as a motivational challenge for assimilated students, racial barriers may undermine expectations for separated students. Psychologists and education professionals should tailor interventions to account for these cultural differences to better support Latinx engineering students' persistence and advancement in the field.
articles.keywords: Acculturation; Psychology; Psychological intervention; Latent growth modeling; Social psychology; Multilevel model; Persistence; Cultural diversity; Structural equation modeling; Vulnerability
articles.summary: 本研究基于文化适应框架，考察了拉丁裔工程本科生的文化取向潜剖面（如双文化型、同化型等）如何调节种族和性别障碍对职业期望的影响。研究发现，性别障碍仅对同化组有显著积极影响，而种族障碍对分离组有显著负面影响，突显了在干预中考虑文化差异的重要性。

articles.title: Revisiting the two-factor model of ethnic-racial and U.S. identity exploration among Hispanic college students.
articles.authors: Alan Meca, Bethany Cruz, Isabella Cruz, Zenetta Hinojosa, Michelle Nguyễn, Melinda Gonzales-Backen
articles.abstract: Establishing a positive ethnic-racial identity (ERI) and U.S. identity (USI) is an important cultural asset capable of promoting positive adjustment. Although exploration has been identified as the fundamental process underlying ERI and USI development, existing research has largely utilized a unidimensional conceptualization. The present study sought to replicate Syed et al.'s (2013) findings, which differentiated between two forms of ERI exploration, search (i.e., reflection/talking to others) and participation (i.e., involvement in events/experiences), and extend to USI.
